name: claude-cognitive-toolkit
description: Evidence-based CBT and DBT intervention skills for AI assistants. Implements 7-step thought records, Opposite Action, DEAR MAN roleplay, TIPP, Wise Mind, and affect labeling — with configurable pushback levels and optional HRV biofeedback integration.
author: Gleb Kalinin

techniques.name: Thought Record
techniques.description: 7-step cognitive restructuring using Burns' distortion taxonomy. Captures situation, automatic thoughts, distortion detection, emotion rating, balanced reframe, and re-rating.

techniques.name: Opposite Action
techniques.description: DBT Emotion Regulation skill. Identifies the action urge driven by a problematic emotion, then guides behaviorally opposite response to break avoidance and shame spirals.

techniques.name: DEAR MAN
techniques.description: DBT Interpersonal Effectiveness skill for assertive communication. Includes AI roleplay mode where the model plays the other person so you can rehearse before the real conversation.

techniques.name: TIPP
techniques.description: DBT Crisis Survival skill: Temperature, Intense exercise, Paced breathing, Paired muscle relaxation. Fast physiological regulation for acute distress.

techniques.name: Chain Analysis
techniques.description: DBT behavior analysis tool. Maps the chain of events, thoughts, emotions, and behaviors leading to a problem behavior to identify intervention points.

techniques.name: Behavioral Activation
techniques.description: CBT technique for depression. Schedules and tracks activities correlated with mood improvement, breaking the inactivity-low mood cycle.

techniques.name: Wise Mind
techniques.description: DBT Core Mindfulness skill. Guides access to the synthesis of Emotion Mind and Reasonable Mind — the intuitive inner wisdom that integrates both.

techniques.name: GIVE / FAST
techniques.description: DBT Interpersonal Effectiveness skills for maintaining relationships (GIVE) and self-respect (FAST) during interactions.

techniques.name: Affect Labeling
techniques.description: Neuroscience-based technique: naming emotions reduces amygdala activation. Brief structured labeling exercise to reduce emotional intensity.
